digest.top_line: TREASURE: adding consolidative thoracic RT to atezo maintenance in ES-SCLC halted early for toxicity; fatal AEs 19.4% vs 3.0% (P=.04), mOS 6.7 vs 13.4mo (HR 1.55, ns).
digest.tldr: Thoracic carried the day's only signal, and it's a caution: consolidative TRT layered onto atezo maintenance in ES-SCLC produced excess fatal SAEs (infection/respiratory), not benefit, and the SMC halted TREASURE at 68/104. The pre-IO CREST rationale for consolidative TRT doesn't transfer to the IO-maintenance setting.

sites.intro: ES-SCLC consolidative TRT: the modest pre-IO benefit does not carry into the immunotherapy-maintenance era, where it added fatal toxicity.

studies.name: TREASURE
studies.tldr: mOS numerically worse with consolidative TRT + atezo vs atezo alone (6.7 vs 13.4mo, HR 1.55 ns); SAEs 61% vs 18%, halted early for safety.
details: 📐 RT: consolidative thoracic radiotherapy 30 Gy in 10 fractions, added to atezolizumab maintenance (arm A)

details.text: 📊 Efficacy + safety by arm (A = TRT+atezo, B = atezo alone)

details: 🔍 Phase 2 open-label RCT, 20 sites Germany/Austria; ES-SCLC with ≥SD after induction carbo-etoposide-atezolizumab
🔍 Planned 104 pts; SMC halted at 68 randomized (34/arm) for excess fatal SAEs in arm A
⚠️ Excess SAEs were infection + respiratory; proposed driver = persistent radiation-induced lymphocyte depletion
⚠️ Lower baseline single-breath DLCO in arm A pts with fatal AEs
⚠️ Baseline characteristics balanced between arms; no other SAE risk factor identified
🔗 vs CREST (pre-IO): modest OS benefit for consolidative TRT after chemo; adding TRT to IO maintenance here gave harm, not benefit
⚠️ Underpowered for OS (P=.34, HR CI 0.90-2.69 crosses 1); the significant signal is toxicity, not the OS point estimate

verdict.rationale: Randomized phase 2 halted early for safety; significant toxicity excess (SAEs, fatal AEs) argues against consolidative TRT + IO, challenging CREST-era extrapolation. Underpowered for OS.
verdict.audience: Unselected extensive-stage SCLC, ≥SD after chemo-IO induction

studies.significance: The RT read is the harm mechanism: infection and respiratory SAEs tracked with persistent post-TRT lymphocyte depletion, and fatal-AE pts had lower baseline DLCO (characteristics otherwise balanced). This argues against extrapolating CREST-era consolidative TRT into the atezo-maintenance setting for unselected ES-SCLC; weigh DLCO before offering it.
studies.monday_clinic: In unselected ES-SCLC with residual thoracic disease after chemo-IO induction, this questions routinely adding consolidative TRT to atezolizumab maintenance; it does not identify the selected, better-DLCO pt who might tolerate it.

primary_endpoint.name: Overall survival
primary_endpoint.stat_detail: HR 1.55 (95% CI 0.90-2.69), P=.34; primary not met, favors atezo alone

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: **Phase 2 open-label RCT**, 1:1, 20 sites (Germany/Austria). Planned 104 pts; SMC **halted recruitment at 68** (34/arm) for excess fatal SAEs in arm A. Recruited Sept 2020-Aug 2022.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: **ES-SCLC with ≥ stable disease** after induction carboplatin-etoposide-atezolizumab. **Unselected**, no pulmonary-function or biomarker gate.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: Arm A added **consolidative thoracic RT, 30 Gy in 10 fractions**, to atezolizumab maintenance. Arm B: **atezolizumab maintenance alone**.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: **Primary OS not met**; combination arm numerically worse. Toxicity markedly higher (arm-vs-arm table above).

analysis_sections.label: Safety
analysis_sections.body: **SAEs 61.3% vs 18.2%** (P<.001); **fatal AEs 19.4% vs 3.0%** (P=.04), predominantly infection and respiratory. Persistent **post-TRT lymphocyte depletion** proposed as facilitator; lower baseline DLCO in fatal-AE pts.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: **CREST** (pre-IO) showed a modest OS benefit for consolidative TRT after chemo; **IMpower133** set chemo-atezo + maintenance. TREASURE added TRT to IO maintenance and found **harm, not benefit**.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents unselected ES-SCLC with ≥ stable disease after chemo-immunotherapy induction. Does not represent selected pts with preserved pulmonary function or a defined low-risk thoracic-disease subgroup.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: **Open-label**; terminated early at 68/104, **underpowered for OS** (P=.34, HR CI crosses 1). Post hoc survival update (April 2026) after April 2025 database lock.

open_questions: Which ES-SCLC pts can receive consolidative TRT without excess toxicity
Does radiation-induced lymphopenia predict TRT harm
Optimal timing of consolidative TRT relative to immunotherapy maintenance

related_trials.brief_title: Lymphocyte Depletion and Change in Lymphocyte Functionality
related_trials.brief_summary: Lymphocytes are a type of white blood cell (WBC) responsible for adaptive immunity. Thoracic tumors are adjacent to many blood/immune rich organs including the great vessels, heart, thoracic-spine, and lymph-node-stations. During radiation treatment the impact to lymphocytes can be significant. This may cause a decrease in the amount of lymphocytes. A researcher at UVA has created a system to predict and reduce the immune cell reduction following lung SBRT treatments beyond standard of care.…

papers.title: Consolidative Thoracic Radiotherapy With Atezolizumab Maintenance in Extensive-Stage Small Cell Lung Cancer
papers.abstract: Importance Chemoimmunotherapy followed by immunotherapy maintenance is the standard first-line treatment for extensive-stage small cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC), yet data regarding efficacy and safety of consolidative thoracic radiotherapy (TRT) are lacking. Objective To determine whether combining consolidative TRT with immunotherapy maintenance in ES-SCLC is safe and improves patients’ overall survival (OS) and progression-free survival (PFS). Design, Settings, and Participants This was a multicenter open-label phase 2 randomized clinical trial recruiting patients from September 2020 to August 2022 in Germany and Austria, with the last follow-up visit in September 2024. Eligible patients had ES-SCLC with at least stable disease after induction chemoimmunotherapy (carboplatin−etoposide−atezolizumab). Although the database was locked in April 2025, a post hoc survival update was performed in April 2026. Data were analyzed from April 2025 to April 2026. Interventions Randomized (1:1) to either atezolizumab maintenance combined with consolidative TRT (30 Gy in 10 fractions) (arm A) or atezolizumab maintenance alone (arm B). Main Outcome and Measure OS (time from randomization to death due to any cause). Results Of 96 patients assessed for eligibility, 68 patients were randomized; recruitment was prematurely terminated due to safety concerns. Median OS in the combination arm was numerically shorter compared to atezolizumab only (6.7 months [95% CI, 5.1-9.0] vs 13.4 months [95% CI, 10.7-17.5]; HR, 1.55 [95% CI, 0.90-2.69]; P = .34), while median PFS was similar (2.4 months [95% CI, 1.3-3.9] vs 2.6 months [95% CI, 1.2-3.9]; HR, 0.92 [95% CI, 0.54-1.55]; P = .85). TRT plus atezolizumab was accompanied by higher frequency of severe adverse events (SAEs) (19 patients [61.3%] vs 6 patients [18.2%]; P &amp;lt; .001) and fatal outcomes (6 patients [19.4%] vs 1 patient [3.0%]; P = .04). Safety analysis revealed predominance of infection and respiratory disorder−related SAEs, possibly facilitated by a depletion of lymphocytes observed specifically in patients after TRT, and by a lower single breath diffuse capacity of the lungs for carbon monoxide in patients with fatal AEs in arm A. No further risk factors were identified, given that baseline characteristics were well balanced between both arms and between patients with and without SAEs, including fatal SAEs. Conclusions and Relevance In this randomized clinical trial, TRT combined with immunotherapy increased toxic effects in unselected patients with ES-SCLC without survival benefit, presumably due to radiation-induced lymphocyte depletion facilitating infections. Cautious patient selection and further investigation to identify those who may benefit without undue risk are required. Trial Registration ClinicalTrials.gov Identifier: NCT04462276
